text: When you write code, you will inevitably find bugs. Identifying bugs is the first step to fixing them. A systematic approach helps you solve problems efficiently. Instead of guessing, start by narrowing down where the error occurs. Many programmers use debugging tools that allow them to pause the code and inspect variables. You can also use logging, which means adding print statements to track what the program is doing at different points. Logging is especially useful for understanding complex or hard-to-reproduce bugs. By following a clear, step-by-step method and using these tools, you can save time and become a more confident programmer.
